prompts.prompt:
Warm, vibrant Mexican restaurant aesthetic. Deep red (#C41E3A) and charcoal (#2D2D2D) brand palette with gold (#F5A623) accents. Bold sans-serif typography, clean and modern. Photography style: close-up food shots with warm golden lighting, rich saturated colors, visible texture on tortillas and grilled meats. Authentic border-style taco shop energy — not corporate, not fast-food. Family-owned warmth with a craft brewery edge. San Diego sunshine and neighborhood pride. Trust-forward, appetite-triggering, real and unpolished.

Clean, high-trust static ad in the style of a premium Google review card. Background: softly blurred photo of a beautifully plated carne asada taco on a handmade corn tortilla with fresh cilantro, diced onion, and a drizzle of vibrant green salsa, with a warm, slightly darkened overlay. Centered card element with white rounded rectangle, subtle drop shadow. Large gold five-star rating (★★★★★) at top. Bold sans-serif headline: "Decent price, great flavor, easy to order, very friendly!". Attribution line below in lighter weight: "— Jessica, San Diego Yelp Review". Business name/logo lockup bottom center: Chachos Tacos. Primary brand color deep red (#C41E3A) used as accent stripe at bottom. Aspect ratio 4:5. Ultra-realistic photography style, professional ad composition.

copy.headline: Decent price, great flavor, easy to order, very friendly!
copy.subhead: — Jessica, San Diego Yelp Review

prompts.notes: Uses top customer review phrase verbatim. Reference image of finished taco dish recommended for background.

prompts.prompt:
Warm, vibrant Mexican restaurant aesthetic. Deep red (#C41E3A) and charcoal (#2D2D2D) brand palette with gold (#F5A623) accents. Bold sans-serif typography, clean and modern. Photography style: close-up food shots with warm golden lighting, rich saturated colors, visible texture on tortillas and grilled meats. Authentic border-style taco shop energy — not corporate, not fast-food. Family-owned warmth with a craft brewery edge. San Diego sunshine and neighborhood pride. Trust-forward, appetite-triggering, real and unpolished.

High-impact before/after split ad. Exact vertical 50/50 split. LEFT SIDE: labeled "BEFORE" in white bold uppercase with subtle dark overlay — shows raw marinated pork on a prep station, uncooked corn masa dough, and fresh uncut vegetables. RIGHT SIDE: labeled "AFTER" in deep red (#C41E3A) bold uppercase — shows a gorgeous plated cochinita pibil burrito cut in half revealing juicy tender pork, with homemade chips and green salsa on the side. Thin white dividing line with small circular badge at center reading "Chachos Tacos". Bottom strip in deep red (#C41E3A): white text "Border-Style Tacos | Clairemont Mesa | chachostacossd.com". Clean, professional, results-focused composition. Aspect ratio 1:1.

copy.headline: From Scratch to Plate
copy.subhead: Border-Style Tacos | Clairemont Mesa | chachostacossd.com

prompts.notes: Before/after showing raw ingredients to finished dish. Best with real prep and plated food photos.

prompts.prompt:

Premium trust-building static ad featuring a real business owner. Background: clean charcoal (#2D2D2D) with subtle linen texture. Left side: professional photo of Manny, the owner of Chachos Tacos, wearing an apron, warm smile, standing in front of the kitchen. Right side: bold typographic treatment. Large display font headline in white: "Every Tortilla. Every Salsa. Every Dish. Handmade. No Shortcuts.". Subhead in lighter weight gold (#F5A623): "My grandma's recipes. My hands. Your table.". copy.headline: Every Tortilla. Every Salsa. Every Dish. Handmade. No Shortcuts.
copy.subhead: My grandma's recipes. My hands. Your table.

prompts.notes: Requires owner headshot. Strong trust angle — family recipes, handmade quality.

Direct response two-part static ad. Top half (60%): dark charcoal (#2D2D2D) overlay with bold empathy-first headline in white: "Tired of Tacos That Taste Like They Came From a Bag?". Smaller italic subhead in gold (#F5A623): "You deserve better than reheated tortillas and jarred salsa.". Horizontal deep red (#C41E3A) divider. copy.headline: Tired of Tacos That Taste Like They Came From a Bag?
copy.subhead: You deserve better than reheated tortillas and jarred salsa.

prompts.notes: Problem/solution framing — positions Chachos against low-quality chain competitors.

prompts.prompt:
Warm, vibrant Mexican restaurant aesthetic. Deep red (#C41E3A) and charcoal (#2D2D2D) brand palette with gold (#F5A623) accents. Bold sans-serif typography, clean and modern. Photography style: close-up food shots with warm golden lighting, rich saturated colors, visible texture on tortillas and grilled meats. Authentic border-style taco shop energy — not corporate, not fast-food. Family-owned warmth with a craft brewery edge. San Diego sunshine and neighborhood pride. Trust-forward, appetite-triggering, real and unpolished.

Direct, conversational objection-handling ad. Clean editorial layout. Background: warm cream/off-white. Bold hook framed as a customer question in italic, larger size, deep red (#C41E3A): "Wait — you're inside a brewery? Do I have to drink beer to eat there?". Below in charcoal (#2D2D2D): honest direct answer paragraph: "Nope. Societe Brewing is our home base, but you don't need to order a beer. Families, kids, solo lunch breaks — everyone's welcome. Walk up, grab tacos, sit on the patio or take it to go. The beer's there if you want it. The tacos are there no matter what.". Small Manny signature sign-off in script. Chachos Tacos name, (619) 587-1086 and chachostacossd.com at bottom in deep red (#C41E3A). Feels like a real person wrote it. Aspect ratio 4:5.

copy.headline: Wait — you're inside a brewery? Do I have to drink beer to eat there?
copy.subhead: Nope. Families, kids, solo lunch breaks — everyone's welcome.

prompts.notes: Addresses the #1 confusion about being located inside Societe Brewing.
